skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar and talking head videos via inference.sh CLI. Recommended: P-Video-Avatar (fastest, cheapest, built-in TTS). Also: OmniHuman, Fabric, PixVerse. Audio: Inworld TTS-2 (100+ languages, emotion steering for characters), ElevenLabs, Kokoro.
skill.summary: Create AI avatar and talking head videos from images using inference.sh CLI
skill.author: skills-shell

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill (SKILL.md) describing how to use the inference.sh belt CLI for AI avatar video generation. All static findings are false positives: the weak crypto flag was triggered by YAML frontmatter, and the backtick executions are markdown code fence delimiters in documentation examples, not actual code execution. The hardcoded URLs are legitimate inference.sh service endpoints and documentation links.

content.user_title: Create AI Avatar and Talking Head Videos
content.value_statement: Creating professional avatar videos requires multiple tools and services. This skill simplifies AI avatar video generation with a unified CLI interface to inference.sh, offering built-in TTS, multiple avatar models, and lip-sync capabilities.
actual_capabilities: Generate talking head videos from portrait images with built-in TTS
Create audio-driven avatars using pre-existing audio files
Produce lip-sync videos with multi-character support
Generate UGC-style content with casual, authentic presenter tones
Dub videos into different languages using transcription and TTS pipelines
Create virtual presenters with emotion steering and style control
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and CLI installation
Video quality depends on input image quality and lighting
Models without built-in TTS require separate audio generation step
Some models have slower processing times and higher costs

use_cases.title: Marketing and UGC Content
use_cases.description: Create engaging UGC-style ads and product demos with AI presenters using built-in TTS. Generate multiple presenter variations for A/B testing.

use_cases.title: Training and Educational Videos
use_cases.description: Produce consistent training content with virtual presenters. Use built-in TTS for quick video generation without separate audio recording.

use_cases.title: Video Localization
use_cases.description: Translate video content across languages by combining transcription, translation, and TTS with avatar lip-sync generation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Avatar Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a talking head video using a portrait image with voice script: "[Your script here]" using P-Video-Avatar with Zephyr voice at 1080p resolution.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick avatar video with built-in TTS

prompt_templates.title: Audio-Driven Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an avatar video from [portrait-url] using the audio file at [audio-url] with OmniHuman 1.5 model.

prompt_templates.title: UGC-Style Presenter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a casual UGC-style avatar video with authentic testimonial tone. Use portrait prompt: 'casual selfie-style photo of a [description]' with voice script: "[natural testimonial text]".

prompt_templates.title: Batch Localization
prompt_templates.prompt: Create [N] presenter variations of this script: "[script]" using different voice options from P-Video-Avatar for multi-language content testing.

output_examples.input: Generate a talking head video using the portrait at example.com/portrait.jpg with the voice script 'Welcome to our product demo. Let me show you three features.'
output_examples.output: A professional talking head video is generated at 1080p resolution with natural lip-sync. The AI presenter delivers the script with the selected voice profile, suitable for embedding in presentations or marketing materials.

output_examples.input: Create UGC-style content with a casual testimonial about a productivity app
output_examples.output: An authentic-looking user testimonial video is created with a casual presenter tone. The video has natural gestures, appropriate lighting, and conversational delivery that feels relatable and genuine.

output_examples.input: Dub a product video into Spanish using the original English audio and a portrait image
output_examples.output: The original video is re-lip-synced with Spanish speech generated from the translated text. The AI avatar maintains consistent appearance while delivering the localized content.

best_practices: Use high-quality, front-facing portrait photos with good lighting for best avatar results
Start with P-Video-Avatar for fastest results and lowest cost before exploring other models
Test voice prompts and video prompts separately to refine tone and appearance before batch generation
anti_patterns: Do not use low-resolution or heavily filtered portraits as input images
Avoid using background-heavy images without proper face cropping
Do not skip testing with a single video before generating batch content

faq.question: What is the best model for creating talking head videos?
faq.answer: P-Video-Avatar is recommended for most use cases. It is 18x faster and 6x cheaper than alternatives while offering built-in TTS, 30 voices, and 1080p support. Use OmniHuman for multi-character scenes or PixVerse for highly realistic lipsync.

faq.question: Do I need to record audio separately?
faq.answer: P-Video-Avatar, Fabric, and OmniHuman offer built-in TTS options. For models without TTS, you can use Inworld TTS-2, ElevenLabs, or Kokoro to generate audio separately before creating the avatar video.

faq.question: What image format works best?
faq.answer: Use clear, front-facing portrait photos with good lighting. The AI works best with professional headshots. Avoid heavily filtered images, group photos without specifying a target character, or low-resolution images.

faq.question: Can I create videos in multiple languages?
faq.answer: Yes. P-Video-Avatar supports 10 languages with built-in TTS. For other languages, generate translated speech using TTS services like Kokoro or Inworld TTS-2, then sync with the avatar using lip-sync models.

faq.question: How do I generate multiple presenter variations?
faq.answer: Use the batch workflow with different voice options (Zephyr, Puck, Aoede) and portrait prompts. Loop through voice variations while keeping the same script to create authentic-looking variations for different audience segments.

faq.question: What is the typical processing time?
faq.answer: P-Video-Avatar processes at approximately 1.83 seconds per second of video. OmniHuman is slower at 28 seconds per second. Processing time depends on model choice, resolution, and video length.
